introSection: Dino Roller (formerly called Dragon Coaster) was a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Flamingo Land in Malton, North Yorkshire, England, UK. It was a standard model from Diego Cavazza. It was removed prior to the 2025 season.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The coaster first opened in 1988 as the Dragon Coaster with blue track and green supports. At some point after 2002, the ride was moved to a spot beside Velocity and Corkscrew and the supports were repainted orange.

For the 2013 season, the ride was relocated to the Primeval area of the park. It was repainted entirely light-brown and renamed Dino Roller. It also featured a large rock in the centre of the ride.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The ride had a single helix.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The train has a dragon theme.

fullText:
Dino Roller (formerly called Dragon Coaster) was a steel kiddie roller coaster located at Flamingo Land in Malton, North Yorkshire, England, UK. It was a standard model from Diego Cavazza. It was removed prior to the 2025 season.

The coaster first opened in 1988 as the Dragon Coaster with blue track and green supports. At some point after 2002, the ride was moved to a spot beside Velocity and Corkscrew and the supports were repainted orange.

For the 2013 season, the ride was relocated to the Primeval area of the park. It was repainted entirely light-brown and renamed Dino Roller. It also featured a large rock in the centre of the ride.

The ride had a single helix.

The train has a dragon theme.
